_notes: Antigravity has TWO subagent models. (1) DYNAMIC subagents: the orchestrator reasons about a /goal and spawns subagents on the fly with isolated context windows; these are NOT user-declared and have no file. (2) FILE-BASED custom subagents in Antigravity CLI: declared via an agent.json file. The official page https://antigravity.google/docs/subagents is JS-rendered and its body was not extractable; the file-based fields below are sourced from a community-confirmed agent.json example (GitHub discussion #27305) and may differ slightly from the unverified official schema. Legacy gemini-cli markdown subagents are NOT compatible with antigravity-cli.

agentFile.format: json (agent.json). Dynamic subagents are UI/orchestrator-only with no file.
agentFile.bodyIs: n/a — agent.json has no markdown body; the system prompt is carried in the config.customAgent.systemPromptSections[].content field, not a document body.

name.description: Subagent identifier; matches the containing folder name (e.g. love_poet).

description.description: Human/orchestrator-facing description of what the subagent specializes in.

hidden.description: Visibility flag controlling whether the subagent is surfaced/selectable.

config.customAgent.systemPromptSections.description: The subagent's system prompt, expressed as ordered titled sections (e.g. title 'Agent System Instructions', content = the instructions). This is the system-prompt mechanism — it is a field, not a markdown body.

config.customAgent.toolNames.allowed: tool ids e.g. send_message, find_by_name, grep_search, view_file, list_dir, read_url_content, search_web, schedule
config.customAgent.toolNames.description: Allowlist of tools the subagent may invoke.

config.customAgent.systemPromptConfig.in.allowed: user_information, mcp_servers, skills, subagent_reminder, messaging, artifacts, user_rules
config.customAgent.systemPromptConfig.in.description: Which built-in system-prompt sections (context blocks) to inject into the subagent's prompt, including mcp_servers and skills context.

model.description: unverified — no model field appears in the confirmed agent.json example; the official schema may include one.

bashInjection.description: No documented mechanism to inject shell command output into the system prompt at load time.

fileInjection.description: No documented file-include token within agent.json; systemPromptConfig.includeSections injects predefined context blocks, not arbitrary files.

argumentTokens.description: No documented positional/argument placeholder tokens for subagent definitions.

toolReference.description: Tools are referenced by name in the toolNames array; this allowlists the tools the subagent can call.

mcpReference.description: MCP server context is injected by including the 'mcp_servers' section; MCP servers themselves are configured at the CLI level, not in agent.json.

variableTemplating.description: No documented variable/templating syntax inside agent.json content fields.

agentMention.description: Invocation/delegation is performed by the orchestrator (which spawns or selects subagents to run subtasks in parallel with isolated context); no documented @mention token for explicitly addressing a named subagent.
